title: 2022: Tanker's Freight Forecast
summary:
Main
By Arthur Richier
In last year's freight forecast we described 2020 as one of the most volatile years on record. 2021 saw a more tepid year, with freight rates overall languishing at their lowest levels in years. Whilst oil demand continued to recover, helped by a global vaccination drive and re-opening economies, this has not been sufficient to jumpstart a morose tanker market.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
By Arthur Richier
In last year's freight forecast we described 2020 as one of the most volatile years on record. 2021 saw a more tepid year, with freight rates overall languishing at their lowest levels in years.

children.title: 2021: A Year to Forget for Freight Markets
section_path: 2021: A Year to Forget for Freight Markets
children.section_path_text: 2021: A Year to Forget for Freight Markets
children.summary: It is not an understatement to say that freight market participants were fairly optimistic that they had seen the worst of pandemic-related impacts in 2020 and that 2021 would bring about a strong recovery across tanker markets. It was uneven to say the least.

children.title: A Positive Momentum for Freight Rates
section_path: A Positive Momentum for Freight Rates
children.section_path_text: A Positive Momentum for Freight Rates

Image reference: ch1.jpg.png | assets/2022-01-21_2022-the-freight-forecast_img_ch1jpg_0d9d6c4a3bab.png
Freight rates vs. 2021 averages across major vessel classes benchmark routes
Throughout 2021, freight rates remained weak.

children.title: Demand: Is there enough fuel for recovery left in the crude market?
section_path: Demand: Is there enough fuel for recovery left in the crude market?
children.section_path_text: Demand: Is there enough fuel for recovery left in the crude market?

Image reference: ch2.png | assets/2022-01-21_2022-the-freight-forecast_img_ch2_5785ca8cee21.png
Crude/dirty tanker tonne-miles vs. 2019 average by vessel class
Without a doubt crude/dirty tanker segments have been hit the hardest in 2021.

children.title: Demand: Refined products demand kept clean tankers afloat
section_path: Demand: Refined products demand kept clean tankers afloat

Image reference: ch3.png | assets/2022-01-21_2022-the-freight-forecast_img_ch3_bbd69dfa202a.png
CPP tanker tonne-miles vs. 2019 average by vessel class
Clean tanker segments have fared better throughout 2021 than their dirty counterparts.

children.title: Supply: Overcapacity will keep a lid on rates in 2022
section_path: Supply: Overcapacity will keep a lid on rates in 2022
children.section_path_text: Supply: Overcapacity will keep a lid on rates in 2022
children.summary:
Image reference: ch4.png | assets/2022-01-21_2022-the-freight-forecast_img_ch4_403527c9d12f.png
Net fleet additions by class vs. average fleet speed
When it comes to fleet supply, 2021 saw a net addition of 109 vessels across the 5 main tanker classes in focus, equivalent to approximately 3% of total fleet growth.
